Saturday, April 25, 2026 Daily Digest

GPT-5.5 Eyes "Super App" Vision, Adobe + NVIDIA Launch Agentic Marketing, 80% of CEOs Brace for AI Overhaul

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5, Moves Toward "Super App"

OpenAI launched GPT-5.5, which co-founder Greg Brockman calls "a real step forward towards more agentic and intuitive computing." The bigger story: OpenAI's vision of combining ChatGPT, Codex, and AI browser into a unified enterprise "super app" — a single AI surface for everything. This signals where the interface layer is heading: AI as the operating system, not just another tool.

Read on TechCrunch →
2

Adobe + NVIDIA + WPP Launch CX Enterprise Coworker

Adobe Summit revealed an expanded collaboration with NVIDIA and WPP to bring agentic AI to enterprise marketing. The new "CX Enterprise Coworker" — powered by NVIDIA's OpenShell runtime and Nemotron models — enables autonomous agents that can "plan, create, produce and activate content continuously" while maintaining brand governance. This is the clearest signal yet that AI agents are becoming the default for creative ops at scale.

Read on NVIDIA Blog →
3

Gartner: 80% of CEOs Say AI Will Force Operational Overhaul

New Gartner survey of 469 CEOs reveals a seismic shift in thinking. The headline: 80% expect AI to force "high to medium degree of change" to operational capabilities. By 2028, only 13% expect to remain at task-level automation — 32% expect AI to assist human decision-making, while 27% expect operations "primarily without human intervention." The era of autonomous business is no longer theoretical.

Read on TechEdge AI →
4

DeepSeek-V4 Preview Launches on Huawei Chips

DeepSeek, whose low-cost AI model stunned the world last year, dropped a preview of V4 adapted for Huawei chip technology — with million-token context windows. China's AI self-sufficiency is accelerating. For enterprise tech watchers, this matters: the AI race is no longer just OpenAI vs Anthropic. Global competition is reshaping who builds the foundation models enterprise software runs on.

Read on Reuters →
5

Gumloop Raises $50M for AI Workflow Automation

Gumloop, the visual AI workflow builder used by Shopify, Instacart, and Webflow, just raised a $50M Series B led by Benchmark. Their pitch: connect your favorite tools with the latest LLMs on a visual canvas, no code required. It's another sign that the "workflow automation" layer between AI models and business tools is becoming the battleground.

Read on Gumloop →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Gartner CEO survey. The numbers are stark: 27% of CEOs expect to operate "primarily without human intervention" by 2028. That's not a pilot — that's a business model shift. The question isn't whether AI will transform enterprise operations; it's whether you'll be the one leading that transformation or adapting to someone else's.
